description: Category array refactor + automatic memory consolidation (dedup, expire, prune, balance-aware scheduling)

userStories.title: Schema change: category → categories in Memory.Info and MemoryRecord
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the data model to support multiple categories per memory so that knowledge spanning multiple domains is correctly classified.
acceptanceCriteria: Memory.Category enum unchanged (still the 7 values: style, pattern, tool, domain, workflow, correction, context)
Memory.Info: field `category` replaced by `categories: z.array(Memory.Category).min(1)`
Memory.CreateInput updated: `categories` is required, accepts array
MemoryRecord in storage.ts: field `category: string` replaced by `categories: string[]`
All TypeScript compilation errors from the rename are resolved across the codebase
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 1: Category Array Refactor. This is the foundation — all other stories depend on it. Focus only on type definitions, not migration or consumer updates.

userStories.title: Storage migration: read-time normalization and dual-field write
userStories.description: As a user with existing memories, I need my personal.json data to be seamlessly migrated so nothing breaks on upgrade or downgrade.
acceptanceCriteria: Read-time normalization in Memory.get(), Memory.list(), and storage loadAll(): if raw record has `category` (string) and no `categories` (array), set `categories = [category]`
Mixed-format resilience: if a record has both `category` and `categories`, prefer `categories`
Write-time: MemoryStorage.save() always writes `categories` array field, also writes `category: categories[0]` as deprecated compatibility field for downgrade safety
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This enables the code to read old-format personal.json without breaking. The bulk migration is a separate story.

userStories.title: Bootstrap bulk migration for categories
userStories.description: As a user, I need a one-time bulk migration so that repeated per-read normalization is eliminated after first startup.
acceptanceCriteria: A migrateCategories() function bulk-converts all records in personal.json: for each record, converts category → categories: [category], writes both fields
Called during InstanceBootstrap (alongside existing recovery logic), guarded by meta key `categoriesMigratedAt` to run only once
Idempotent — running twice produces identical output, no data loss
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Optimizer hook: categories.includes('correction') guard
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the optimizer hook's correction guard and team candidate logic to work with the categories array.
acceptanceCriteria: optimizer-hook.ts: `memory.category === 'correction'` changed to `memory.categories.includes('correction')`
No other optimizer logic depends on category field directly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Small, focused change. Must happen early because optimizer runs on session.created.

userStories.title: Injection grouping: group by categories[0]
userStories.description: As a user, I want the <memory> block in system prompts to still be grouped by category, with multi-category memories appearing under their primary (first) category.
acceptanceCriteria: injector.ts block(): group by `categories[0]` (primary) — a memory appears in exactly one group
Output format unchanged for single-category memories
`order` array unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extractor schema and prompt: output categories array
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the extractor to output and parse categories arrays, with backward compat for old-format LLM responses.
acceptanceCriteria: ExtractedItem schema: accepts both `categories` (array, primary) and `category` (string, legacy fallback). Zod .transform() normalizes to `categories`
Extract prompt (extract.txt): instructs LLM to output `"categories": ["style", "pattern"]`
Memory.create() calls in extractor use `categories` field
formatExisting() updated: `- [id] (style, pattern) content`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Recall prompt: pass categories array to LLM
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the recall prompt to show categories arrays so the recall LLM can filter correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: recall.ts: passes `categories: m.categories` instead of `category: m.category` to recall LLM
Recall prompt (recall.txt): input format shows categories array
Remember prompt (remember.txt): updated wording to show categories parameter
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tool interface: memory_remember, memory_list, memory_forget support categories
userStories.description: As a user (or LLM calling tools), I want memory tools to support multi-category with backward compat for old category parameter.
acceptanceCriteria: memory_remember: accepts `categories` (array, primary) and `category` (string, deprecated). Normalized internally to categories array
memory_list: `category` filter param unchanged (single value) — matches any memory where categories.includes(value)
memory_list display: `[style,pattern]` for multi-category, `[style]` for single
memory_forget display: `Forgot: style,pattern` or `Forgot: ${categories.join(',')}`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: REST API backward compat: accept both category and categories
userStories.description: As an external SDK consumer, I need the API to continue working during the transition so my integration doesn't break on upgrade.
acceptanceCriteria: REST API GET / (list): accepts query param `category` (string, deprecated) and `categories` (string, comma-separated). Both filter by categories.includes(value)
REST API PUT /:id (update): accepts body field `category` (string, deprecated) and `categories` (array). If both present, categories wins
REST API response body: includes both `category: categories[0]` (deprecated) and `categories: [...]`
REST API GET /stats: categoryBreakdown counts each memory once per category it belongs to
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Web UI: multi-category badges and filter
userStories.description: As a user of the Memory Manager web UI, I want to see and filter by multiple categories.
acceptanceCriteria: CategoryBadge component renders multiple badges for multi-category memories
Category filter dropdown unchanged (single select) — matches any category in the array
Edit modal allows adding/removing categories (multi-select)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Regenerate JS SDK after schema change
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SDK types regenerated so external consumers get updated TypeScript definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Run ./packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts successfully
Generated types in sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts include both `category` (deprecated) and `categories` fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Test suite update for categories array
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all existing tests to pass after the category→categories migration.
acceptanceCriteria: test/memory/web-api.test.ts: all `category: '...'` in test data changed to `categories: ['...']`; filter and stats assertions updated
test/memory/tools.test.ts: all `category:` changed to `categories:`, all `m.category` assertions changed to `m.categories`
test/session/agent-request.test.ts: `Category.options.toContain(item.category)` updated to validate against categories array
test/session/hooks/ralph-loop.test.ts: test helper `category:` changed to `categories:`
All tests pass: `bun test` in packages/opencode
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: MemoryEvent.Consolidated event definition
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single event summarizing all consolidation actions so downstream systems can display consolidation activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Define MemoryEvent.Consolidated in event.ts with schema: { deduped: number, expired: number, pruned: number, total_removed: number }
Define MemoryEvent.ConsolidationEligible in event.ts with schema: { credits: number, provider: string }
Published once at end of consolidation run if total_removed > 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2: Memory Consolidation. Starting here, stories build on the categories array foundation.

userStories.title: Config schema: consolidation section in config.memory
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure consolidation behavior through the standard config file.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `consolidation` section to config.memory schema in config.ts with fields: enabled (bool, default true), interval (number ms, default 14400000), similarity (number, default 0.8), prune_threshold (number, default 0.1), prune_age (number days, default 60), reset_day (1-28, default 1), days_before_reset (number, default 3), min_spare_credits (number USD, default 0.50)
All fields optional with sensible defaults
Existing config.memory.autoOptimize: false also disables consolidation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Heuristic deduplication logic
userStories.description: As a user, I want duplicate memories to be automatically merged so my memory pool stays clean without manual effort.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement consolidate() function (in decay.ts or new optimizer.ts alongside maintain()) that scans all personal memories for near-duplicate pairs using Jaccard similarity >= config.memory.consolidation.similarity (default 0.8)
For each duplicate group: keep the entry with higher score, merge useCount, hitCount, tags (union), and categories (union) from removed entries into the kept entry, then batch delete the duplicates
Dedup runs across category boundaries — comparison is content-only, ignores categories
Log each merge: { kept: id, removed: [ids], reason: 'dedup', similarity: number }
Consolidation is idempotent — running twice in a row produces no changes on the second run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expired memory cleanup and low-score pruning
userStories.description: As a user, I want expired and low-value memories to be automatically removed so the pool doesn't fill with dead entries.
acceptanceCriteria: During consolidation, delete all memories where expiresAt is set and expiresAt < Date.now(). Log each: { id, reason: 'expired', expiresAt }
After decay score update, delete memories matching ALL of: score < prune_threshold (default 0.1), useCount === 0, hitCount === 0, age > prune_age days (default 60)
Do NOT prune memories with inject: true (manually pinned)
Do NOT prune memories with scope: 'team'
Do NOT prune memories whose categories includes 'correction' (correction memories are exempt regardless of score)
Do NOT prune memories with status: 'confirmed' and hitCount > 0
Log each removal: { id, reason: 'low-score', score, age }
All removal operations use Memory.batchRemove() for atomic persistence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Unified consolidation trigger via Scheduler
userStories.description: As a user, I want consolidation to have multiple trigger points but be rate-limited to run at most once per interval, so idle periods are utilized without excessive execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Register a Scheduler task 'memory-consolidation' with scope 'instance' and interval from config.memory.consolidation.interval (default 4h)
The Scheduler task is the sole executor of consolidation logic (dedup + expire + prune). It calls the consolidate() function from US-015/US-016
Rate-limited via single shared meta key Memory.getMeta('lastConsolidateAt') — skip if last run was within consolidation.interval

The existing optimizer-hook.ts (session.created) no longer runs consolidation directly. It only ensures the Scheduler task is registered (idempotent). Optimizer hook continues to run decay, confirmation, and team-candidate detection independently
Consolidation failures in one step (e.g. dedup) do not block subsequent steps (e.g. expire, prune) — each step has independent try/catch
Publish MemoryEvent.Consolidated with counts at end of run if total_removed > 0
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Provider.balance() for OpenRouter credit query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a generic interface to query the user's remaining credit balance from their LLM provider.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Provider.balance(providerID: string): Promise<{ credits: number, unit: string } | undefined> that returns remaining credits or undefined if provider doesn't support balance queries

Implement for OpenRouter: GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/auth/key with user's API key, parse data.usage and data.limit, return { credits: limit - usage, unit: 'usd' }. If limit is null (unlimited), return undefined
Return undefined for all other providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Bedrock, etc.)
Cache result for 10 minutes using simple in-memory cache with TTL keyed by providerID
Balance query failures (network error, auth error) are logged and treated as undefined — never block consolidation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Balance-aware consolidation trigger (log only)
userStories.description: As a user with a subscription that resets monthly, I want the system to detect spare credits near billing reset and log the opportunity for future LLM-based consolidation.
acceptanceCriteria: In the Scheduler task from US-017, after heuristic consolidation, check provider balance via Provider.balance()

If balance is available AND credits > config.memory.consolidation.min_spare_credits (default 0.50 USD) AND today is within config.memory.consolidation.days_before_reset days of the configured reset_day: log 'spare credits detected, consolidation eligible'
Publish MemoryEvent.ConsolidationEligible event with { credits, provider }
Phase 1 only: no actual LLM consolidation is triggered — this is observability prep for future phase
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the final story. It prepares the hook point for future LLM-based consolidation but does not invoke it.
